description_long: This sculpture depicts two human figures seated closely together on a rough, block-like base, their forms intertwined in an intimate embrace. The figures are rendered in a simplified, almost abstract style, with smooth, rounded contours and minimal facial detail. The surface of the sculpture is mottled and lightly textured, suggesting it is carved from a single piece of pale stone, likely marble. The figures' arms and legs are gently curved, and their bodies are fused at several points, emphasizing unity and connection. The base is irregular and unfinished, contrasting with the more refined forms of the figures above. The sculpture is set on a patch of earth with a ring of grass, indicating an outdoor installation.
description: A pale stone sculpture of two abstracted human figures embracing while seated on a rough, blocky base, with smooth, rounded forms and minimal detail.
description_view_0: The view shows the two figures from the side, highlighting their close embrace and the curvature of their bodies, with the left figure's arm draped around the other.
description_view_1: This angle presents a more frontal view, revealing the chest and face of the left figure and the overlapping arms and legs, emphasizing their intimate pose.
description_view_2: The rear view displays the backs of both figures, their forms merging into the blocky base, with the embrace visible as a single, unified mass.
description_view_3: This view shows the figures from the opposite side, focusing on the back and side of the right figure and the rough, unfinished texture of the base.
attribute_location_context: The sculpture is installed outdoors on a patch of earth surrounded by grass, suggesting a park or garden setting.
